title: Merck — HIV — briefing Q3-2025
html:
What Changed in HIV (Merck) — Q3 2025

Generated: 2026-04-24 (retrospective) Quarter covered: July – September 2025 Data sources: Merck Q3 2025 earnings, ZoomRx HIV Sampling Preference Study (Jul 2025), Phase 3 clinical readouts, DHHS guidelines Reading time: ~10 minutes

1. TL;DR (5 Bullets)

  1. ZoomRx Sampling Preference Study completed (July 2025) — 127 HCPs surveyed on optimal sample distribution strategy ahead of DOR/ISL launch. Sales rep delivery wins (55% vs 45% mail), with Merck target list HCPs indexing 17% higher on rep preference.
  2. Merck HIV Investor Event (June 2025) — Merck disclosed a $15B commercial opportunity for HIV portfolio by mid-2030s, anchored by DOR/ISL (IDVYNSO) and islatravir-based combinations. This is Merck's clearest signal yet that HIV is a strategic growth platform, not a maintenance business.
  3. Biktarvy continues to dominate sampling — 90% penetration, primarily rep-delivered. This is the benchmark IDVYNSO must match at launch.
  4. Dovato PASO DOBLE 96-week data reported — Dovato matched Biktarvy with significantly less weight gain over 2 years. Validates the "fewer drugs, less weight gain" positioning that IDVYNSO will also pursue.
  5. Pifeltro/Delstrigo remain small — combined sub-$500M globally; IDVYNSO is the make-or-break product for Merck's HIV franchise.

2. Data Events That Matter

ZoomRx Sampling Preference Study — Launch-Critical Intelligence

  • What happened: 127 HCPs (IDs, PCPs, HIV specialists) surveyed on how they prefer to receive HIV treatment and PrEP samples. 55% prefer sales rep delivery overall; 64% among Merck's target list HCPs.
  • What it means: This is the only external dataset on HIV sampling dynamics. It directly informs Merck's IDVYNSO distribution strategy. Key finding: "Patients can start treatment right away" (60%) and "ability to ask questions" (60%) are the top benefits of rep delivery — both align with Merck's improving rep quality scores.
  • What Vinoth should say: "We ran the only study that quantifies HCP sampling preferences in HIV. Your target list physicians prefer rep delivery by 17 points. For IDVYNSO launch, sales rep delivery should be the primary channel, with mail as a supplemental option for PCPs."

Merck's $15B HIV Opportunity Framing

  • What happened: At Merck's HIV Investor Event (June 2025), management positioned the HIV portfolio as a potential $15B commercial opportunity by mid-2030s, spanning DOR/ISL, islatravir + lenacapavir (oral weekly), and islatravir + ulonivirine.
  • What it means: Merck is telling the Street that HIV is no longer a side business — it's a growth engine. This increases internal resource allocation and field force investment for IDVYNSO launch.
  • What Vinoth should say: "Merck just told investors HIV is a $15 billion franchise. That means they'll invest heavily in IDVYNSO launch — and they'll need external measurement to prove it's working. ZoomRx should be positioned as the primary tracking partner."

Dovato PASO DOBLE Results

  • What happened: 96-week results showed Dovato was as effective as Biktarvy in maintaining suppression, with significantly less weight gain and fewer drug-related adverse events.
  • What it means: ViiV has validated the "2-drug regimen with fewer side effects" positioning that IDVYNSO will also pursue. But Dovato is INSTI-based — IDVYNSO is the only non-INSTI option, adding differentiation.

3. Competitor Earnings Intelligence

Revenue Snapshot — Q3 2025

Company Brand Q3 2025 Revenue YoY Growth Key Signal
Gilead Biktarvy ~$3.3B +8% Undisputed treatment standard; ~70% of new treatment starts
Gilead Sunlenca (lenacapavir) ~$50M N/A (ramp) PURPOSE 1: 100% HIV prevention efficacy; transforming PrEP landscape
GSK/ViiV Cabenuva ~$350M +30% Leading long-acting injectable treatment; every-2-months dosing
GSK/ViiV Dovato ~$450M Stable 2-drug oral leader; PASO DOBLE weight advantage vs Biktarvy
GSK/ViiV Apretude ~$100M Growing Long-acting PrEP (IM q2 months); emerging Sunlenca competition
Pifeltro/Delstrigo <$500M combined Flat Context: Merck's HIV franchise is niche; IDVYNSO is the strategic pivot

Gilead Sciences — Biktarvy ($3.3B, +8%) + Sunlenca ($50M)

Gilead Q3 2025 call (October 2025):

  1. Biktarvy's dominance is structural, not promotional: At ~$3.3B quarterly, Biktarvy captures ~70% of new HIV treatment starts in the US. Gilead's Q3 commentary emphasized that Biktarvy's position is reinforced by long-term real-world data (5+ years), guideline primacy, and the simplicity of the one-pill-once-daily regimen. This is the benchmark IDVYNSO must compete against.

  2. Sunlenca (lenacapavir) PURPOSE 1 data is the most significant HIV advance in a decade: Gilead reported that PURPOSE 1 showed 100% efficacy in HIV prevention — zero infections in the lenacapavir arm. This twice-yearly subcutaneous injection redefines what's possible in PrEP. While Sunlenca revenue is only ~$50M (treatment indication), the PrEP label expansion will be transformative.

  3. Gilead's total HIV franchise is $5B quarterly ($20B annual): Biktarvy + Descovy + Sunlenca + Trodelvy and other HIV assets combined give Gilead an unmatched commercial infrastructure. Gilead's HIV salesforce reach of ~80% HCPs/month dwarfs Merck's 65%.

  4. Gilead positioning lenacapavir for both prevention AND treatment: Management signaled that lenacapavir's long-acting profile could eventually be offered as a twice-yearly treatment option, disrupting the entire oral daily treatment paradigm that IDVYNSO must succeed within.

Implication for Merck: Gilead's Q3 signals are sobering for Merck's HIV ambitions. Biktarvy's 70% new-start share and Gilead's 80% HCP reach represent the structural barrier IDVYNSO must overcome. The lenacapavir PrEP data is a longer-term concern — if the market shifts toward long-acting prevention and treatment, oral daily options (including IDVYNSO) face relevance questions. Merck's near-term play is the "oral simplicity" argument: not everyone wants injections, not every clinic has injection infrastructure.

GSK/ViiV Healthcare — Cabenuva ($350M, +30%) + Dovato ($450M) + Apretude (~$100M)

GSK Q3 2025 call (October 2025):

  1. Cabenuva is the current long-acting treatment leader at ~$350M, +30%: ViiV highlighted Cabenuva's position as the only approved long-acting injectable treatment for HIV. Every-2-months dosing is gaining traction, particularly among patients who struggle with daily oral adherence. However, Cabenuva requires clinic visits for IM injection — a logistical friction that oral regimens don't face.

  2. Dovato's PASO DOBLE 96-week data validates the 2-drug weight advantage: ViiV presented 2-year data showing Dovato matched Biktarvy on efficacy with significantly less weight gain. This is directly relevant to IDVYNSO — Merck will make a similar weight/metabolic argument as a 2-drug regimen. The difference: Dovato is INSTI-based (dolutegravir), while IDVYNSO is non-INSTI.

  3. ViiV's "long-acting is the future" narrative is intensifying: Management framed Cabenuva as "the beginning" and pointed to next-generation long-acting formulations (less frequent dosing, different routes) as the trajectory. This positions oral daily treatment — IDVYNSO's mode of delivery — as the current standard being disrupted.

  4. Apretude (~$100M) is growing in PrEP but faces Sunlenca competition: ViiV's long-acting PrEP product (every 2 months IM) is growing, but Sunlenca's PURPOSE 1 data (twice-yearly, 100% efficacy) threatens to leapfrog Apretude's convenience advantage.

Implication for Merck: ViiV/GSK's messaging creates a two-front challenge for IDVYNSO. On one side, Dovato competes directly in the oral 2-drug space (same "fewer drugs, fewer side effects" positioning). On the other, Cabenuva represents the long-acting alternative that makes the "convenience" argument cut both ways — oral daily is simpler than oral daily, but less convenient than every-2-months injection for some patients. Merck's differentiation must lean hard on non-INSTI mechanism and tenofovir-free profile.

Synthesis: What the Combined Q3 2025 HIV Picture Tells Us

  1. The HIV treatment market is a Gilead duopoly-in-the-making. Biktarvy (~$3.3B/quarter) + lenacapavir (emerging) give Gilead coverage across oral daily AND long-acting injectable. ViiV is the #2 with Cabenuva + Dovato. Merck is a distant third. IDVYNSO must capture share from both leaders, not just one.

  2. Lenacapavir's 100% PrEP efficacy changes the HIV landscape more than any treatment advance. If twice-yearly prevention dramatically reduces new infections, the HIV treatment market itself contracts over a generation. Merck's $15B HIV opportunity depends on the treatment market staying large — lenacapavir PrEP success, paradoxically, threatens that assumption.

  3. The "oral daily" segment is under pressure from both directions. Long-acting injectables (Cabenuva, lenacapavir) are pulling adherence-challenged patients away from oral daily. Meanwhile, within oral daily, Biktarvy's 70% share leaves little room. IDVYNSO's window is the patient who wants oral daily but NOT Biktarvy — INSTI-averse, weight-concerned, renal-concerned patients.

  4. Merck's sampling and SFE data are the best launch intelligence available. ZoomRx's sampling preference study (55% prefer rep delivery, 64% among Merck targets) and the improving rep performance metrics give Merck a data-backed launch playbook. The question is whether clinical differentiation can overcome structural SOV disadvantage.

4. Commercial Pulse

Metric Pifeltro Delstrigo Biktarvy Dovato
Market Position Niche NNRTI add-on 3-drug STR, declining Dominant (#1 prescribed) #2, growing in switch
SOV (Merck overall) 28% Combined with PIF 42% (Gilead) 30% (ViiV)
Rep Reach ~65% HCPs/month — ~80% HCPs/month ~70% HCPs/month
Key Q3 Event Sampling study completed — Continued dominance PASO DOBLE data

Missing data: Exact Q3 2025 revenue for Pifeltro/Delstrigo (not disclosed separately in Merck earnings, bundled in Other Pharmaceuticals).

What Merck management said on the Q3 2025 call:

  1. HIV was not a major earnings focus — the Q3 commentary mentioned doravirine/islatravir only in the pipeline section, reinforcing that pre-approval HIV products do not yet command management airtime.
  2. The June 2025 HIV Investor Event framing ($15B commercial opportunity by mid-2030s) was not repeated on the Q3 earnings call — suggesting the investor event was the dedicated HIV narrative moment, with quarterly calls reserved for revenue-generating assets.
  3. No HIV-specific revenue figures were disclosed separately. Pifeltro/Delstrigo remain bundled in "Other Pharmaceuticals." This lack of visibility is itself a signal: HIV does not yet warrant dedicated reporting.

Key analyst Q&A signals:

  1. Analysts did not ask a single HIV-specific question on the Q3 call. This reflects the Street's view that Merck's HIV franchise is pre-revenue (for the strategic asset, IDVYNSO) and immaterial (for the existing Pifeltro/Delstrigo portfolio).
  2. Gilead's dominance was implicitly acknowledged by the absence of competitive positioning. Merck management did not reference Biktarvy, lenacapavir, or the competitive landscape — suggesting the HIV strategy is being managed quietly until the NDA filing creates a news cycle.

5. Thesis Update

Thesis Status Key Evidence This Quarter
1. Niche vs platform framing NOT YET APPLICABLE IDVYNSO not yet approved; Pifeltro still in niche mode
2. Gilead SOV dominance UNCHANGED Gilead maintains 42% SOV; Merck steady at 28%
3. Oral > injectable UNCHANGED No new injectable data this quarter; Cabenuva growing steadily
4. Sampling as launch lever STRENGTHENED Sampling study directly supports rep delivery as primary channel; 90% Biktarvy penetration is the benchmark
5. Islatravir perception risk NOT YET APPLICABLE No islatravir-containing product on market yet

6. What to Watch Next Quarter (Q4 2025)

  1. DOR/ISL Phase 3 data readouts — treatment-naive and switch data expected; these support the NDA filing
  2. Merck NDA filing for DOR/ISL — NDA acceptance would set the PDUFA date and make the launch timeline concrete
  3. ZoomRx HIV MR Study Wave 2 (Aug-Nov/Dec '25) — Pifeltro and Delstrigo SFE tracking continues; this is the last wave before the DOR/ISL transition
  4. Cabenuva quarterly growth — ViiV/GSK is projecting Cabenuva will reach ~1/3 of HIV revenue by 2026; if it accelerates, injectable share erosion of oral becomes a concern
  5. Lenacapavir PrEP data and treatment trial enrollment — Gilead's twice-yearly injectable is the long-term disruptor; track trial progress

Back to TA Knowledge Hub | Home

Pifeltro/Delstrigo remain small — combined sub-$500M globally; IDVYNSO is the make-or-break product for Merck's HIV franchise.
